presentation_title: Improving Advanced Minimally Invasive Surgical Fellowship Matching Through In-Person Laparoscopic Skills Testing
institution: Houston Methodist Hospital, Division of Minimally Invasive General Surgery
summary: This is an educational short oral session presenting research findings on the use of structured in-person laparoscopic skills testing (FLS Tasks 1 and 5) as part of fellowship candidate evaluation for a minimally invasive surgery program. The presentation includes background, methodology, results, and conclusions but does NOT contain actual video footage of task performance being scored.
key_content_covered: 28-year history of MIS fellowship program with increasing case complexity (30-40% redo procedures)
Mismatch between incoming fellow skills and program requirements
Redesigned matching process incorporating structured interview (SurgWise framework) and in-person laparoscopic skills testing using locally developed MADSkills trainer box
FLS Task 1 (Peg Transfer) and FLS Task 5 (Intracorporeal Suture with Knot Tying) performed at 0° (on-axis) and >45° (off-axis) stations
Task 1 results: 76.9% (30/39) passed 90° station; 20.5% (8/39) failed at 90°; 2.6% (1/39) failed at 45°
Task 5 results: 38.5% (15/39) failed time limit alone; 61.5% (24/39) failed with time and knot integrity criteria; 76.9% (30/39) failed off-axis at 45°
Faculty satisfaction: No candidates who failed at 0° FLS station were ranked or matched; hard stop policy implemented

Conclusions: Non-technical skills assessed remotely; technical skills require in-person testing; program now implementing earlier training, off-axis practice, and more frequent testing using ATLAS skills drills
cannot_score: This is a research presentation slide deck, not a performance video. No actual task execution is visible. Scoring criteria (completion time, penalties, specific technique observations) cannot be applied.
note_to_user: To score an FLS task performance, provide a video showing a trainee or surgeon actively performing one of the five FLS tasks (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, or Intracorporeal Suture). This presentation discusses FLS testing methodology and results but does not contain scorable performance footage.
